Based on the extensive review of the evidence, I confirm my initial stance. The **ulna**, although playing a crucial role in stabilizing the wrist indirectly via the DRUJ, does not form any direct bony articulation within the radiocarpal joint responsible for the primary movements and structure of the wrist. It participates more in terms of providing stability and preventing excessive rotation rather than active joint construction. Hence, the correct answer remains:
\boxed{B} Disagree - The Ulna is correctly excluded because it doesn't form a direct bony component of the radiocarpal joint, whereas options A (Scaphoid), C (Radius), and D (Triquetral) all play roles in the actual articulatory structure of the wrist joint. Their absence would severely impair normal wrist function.
